DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAR-18-2022 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SI CHEN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO COURT FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND DEMAND FOR INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM PLAINTIFF, AHMED ALGAHIM AND TO REQUEST MONETARY SANCTIONS AND TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION TO COMPEL GRANTED. MOTION TO VACATE THE TRIAL DATE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RE-NOTICE IN DEPARTMENT 206 OF THIS COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT JAMESHFLEMING@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMING; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-30-2022 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Court Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Demand For Inspection Of Documents From Plaintiff, Ahmed Algahim And To Request Monetary Sanctions And To Continue The Trial Date, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration Of Charles R. Roe
Off Calendar Mar-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SI CHEN's Motion To Compel Responses To Court Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Demand For Inspection Of Documents From Plaintiff, Ahmed Algahim And To Request Monetary Sanctions And To Continue The Trial Date.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to compel responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories and demand for inspection of documents is unopposed and is therefore granted, with award of sanctions of $750 against plaintiff Ahmed Algahim and in favor of defendant Si Chen. Plaintiff shall comply within 20 days of date of this Order. Defendant's motion to vacate the trial date is denied without prejudice to re-notice in Department 206 of this Court.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
